creator: johny-freebsd@earthmagic.org
text:
When trying to run "nspluginwrapper -a -i -v" (or any other -i
 	invocation) to wrap the linux-flashplugin9, it segfaults without
 	printing anything.
 
 	After having tried upgrading all my ports (in case it was a .so
 	inconsistency or similar odd thing), the problem still
 	persisted.
 
 	Similar attempts and deinstalling/reinstalling associated ports
 	also had no impact on the problem.
 
 	In the end I resorted to running it under truss(1) to see if I
 	could get any clue as to what might be causing the problem.
 	Running "truss nspluginwrapper -a -i -v" yielded the following
 	output (start truncated):
 
 ...
 open("/usr/local/lib/nspluginwrapper/amd64/freebsd/npwrapper.so",O_RDONLY,020000
 100000) = 5 (0x5)
 lseek(5,0x0,SEEK_END)                            = 130658 (0x1fe62)
 lseek(5,0x0,SEEK_SET)                            = 0 (0x0)
 read(5,"\^?ELF\^B\^A\^A\t\0\0\0\0\0\0\0"...,130658) = 130658 (0x1fe62)
 close(5)                                         = 0 (0x0)
 stat("/usr/local/lib/npapi/linux-flashplugin/libflashplayer.so",{ 
 mode=-rwxr-xr-x ,inode=471151,size=8151148,blksize=4096 }) = 0 (0x0)
 geteuid(0x80096c28f,0x7fffffffe23f,0x3400,0xfeff32ff35373268,0x101010101010101,0
 x8080808080808080) = 4005 (0xfa5)
 stat("/etc/nsswitch.conf",{ 
 mode=-rw-r--r--,inode=49608,size=361,blksize=4096 }
 ) = 0 (0x0)
 geteuid(0x7fffffffd0e0,0x2,0x7fffffffd360,0x0,0x8006c1fe0,0x30) = 4005 
 (0xfa5)
 getpid(0x8008488d4,0x2f,0x2f,0x4,0x7fffffffd0d8,0x7fffffffd0ec) = 1269 
 (0x4f5)
 getsockname(3,{ AF_INET 0.0.0.0:60229 },0x7fffffffcf14) = 0 (0x0)
 SIGNAL 11 (SIGSEGV)
 
 	Since the only reason I could imagine the nspluginwrapper trying
 	to open a socket would be to talk NIS, I tried temporarily
 	disabling NIS in my nsswitch.conf file. This change successfully
 	prevented nspluginwrapper from segfaulting on the next run.
 
 	Half the reason for raising this PR is to share the workaround
 	with others who might find themselves in the same situation as I
 	did - it's not exactly obvious that having NIS enabled causes
 	this failure...

Fix: 

I don't know what the proper fix is, and presently I don't have
 	enough time to dig through the source. As a workaround however,
 	temporarily disabling NIS in nsswitch.conf allows for running
 	nspluginwrapper.
 	NIS can then be immediately re-enabled after that.
How-To-Repeat:  	Enable NIS (assuming you have a NIS server set up)
 	- /etc/rc.conf (nis_client_enable="YES",nisdomainname="yourdomain")
 	- /etc/nsswitch.conf (group: files nis, passwd: nis files)
 
 	Enable the linuxulator
 	- /etc/rc.conf (linux_enable="YES")
 	- /etc/sysctl.conf (compat.linux.osrelease=2.6.16)
 
 	Install emulators/linux_base-fc4 (I experienced the same issue
 	when trying to use -f8, but have not verified the workaround on
 	-f8). I consider it unlikely that the specific linux base has
 	any impact on this particular issue.
 
 	Install www/nspluginwrapper.
 
 	Install www/linux-flashplugin9.
 
 	Attempt to run "nspluginwrapper -a -i -v" (as a regular user).
 	This will now segfault.
